title: AD Taylor-tower P12: sparse high-order tensors via sparse GUW recovery and star-coloring seed directions
goal: Per docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md Frontier P12. Extend the P4 GUW mixed-partial layer with sparse high-order tensor recovery. Build a conservative variable-interaction hypergraph, choose seed directions with star-coloring/Walther-style sparse derivative compression, propagate univariate towers through taylor_propagate(f, x, v, K), and recover only structurally nonzero mixed partials. Store Hessians as sparse symmetric CSR and order>2 tensors as compressed sparse fibers over canonical sorted multi-indices.
acceptance: sparse Hessian of a partially separable function matches dense Hessian/GUW recovery through d
higher-order sparse tensor examples match dense recovery for small tractable m,n
structural-zero detection is conservative: uncertain dependencies are treated as possibly nonzero
propagation-count savings are recorded; small cases can fall back to dense when coloring overhead is not worthwhile
icc ad_depth and sparse-vs-dense metamorphic criteria PASS
campaign_payoff: Lets arbitrary-order multivariate AD scale to many-variable sparse problems instead of defaulting to dense O(m^n) storage and recovery.
